new_classes.label_en: other still wine of fresh grapes (no PDO/PGI), in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 29 90)
new_classes.label_de: anderer Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben (ohne g.U./g.g.A.), in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2204 29 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes without a protected designation of origin (PDO) or protected geographical indication (PGI), presented in containers with a nominal volume exceeding 2 litres, and characterised by a total dry matter (non-volatile substances) content as defined in Additional Note 4A to Chapter 22; non-volatile substances include sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters and salts.

new_classes.label_en: total dry matter threshold quality (Additional Note 4A, Chapter 22)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a wine of fresh grapes consisting of the sum of non-volatile substances (including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters and salts) as specified in Additional Note 4A to Chapter 22 of the Combined Nomenclature, used to determine the applicable CN subheading within 2204 29.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the main discriminating criteria for CN 2204 29 90: (1) wine of fresh grapes (subClassOf WineOfFreshGrapes), (2) presented in containers exceeding 2 L (has part some LargeContainer), (3) absence of PDO or PGI (complement restrictions on quality), and (4) the relevance of total dry matter (non-volatile substances) per Additional Note 4A, modelled via a new TotalDryMatterThreshold quality subclass. Coverage is not complete (score ~0.72) because: (a) the specific numeric thresholds for total dry matter from Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22 are not encoded as datatype range restrictions (the note text references them but the actual numeric values are in the legal notes not reproduced here); (b) the enumerated list of non-volatile substance types (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters, salts) is described in the note but only captured at the class-definition level, not as formal part-of restrictions; (c) the 'other' catch-all nature of 22042990 relative to sibling codes 22042922–22042998 is partially expressed through the PDO/PGI complement restrictions but full sibling disjointness is not formally asserted here.
